title: bumelia lanuginosa subsp. albicans
intro: Bumelia lanuginosa subsp. albicans, also known as the White Bumelia or White Snaggletooth, is a deciduous shrub or small tree native to the southeastern United States. The plant has been used in traditional medicine due to its medicinal properties, including its antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties. The health benefits of Bumelia lanuginosa subsp. albicans include its ability to aid in wound healing, reduce fever, and alleviate respiratory issues such as coughs and colds. The plant can be prepared for herbal use through infusion, decoction, or tincture, and is often used to make teas, salves, and ointments that can be applied topically or ingested.
